title: Give Input on the New Mexico Rio Grande Trail
content:
The Rio Grande Trail is a continuous trail that bisects New Mexico from south to north. And right now, there’s an opportunity for feedback on how to bridge the gap in the current trail alignment between the Counties of Santa Fe and Taos. Through conversations with partners, three draft alignments have been identified. These drafts are for cyclists, equestrians, and backpackers. The Radavist is based in Santa Fe and this our backyard. As stewards of this area, we are reaching out to you – our community – for further input on these draft alignments.

You can take Santa Fe to Taos Alignment Survey to share your ideas AFTER you poke around on the map here. Please, only give feedback if you know the area!

The post Give Input on the New Mexico Rio Grande Trail appeared first on The Radavist.
author: John Watson
